item.title: Iran Proposes Peace Deal With Reparations and US Troop Withdrawal
item.description: Tehran presented a revised peace proposal to the US via Pakistan, demanding reparations for war damage and withdrawal of American forces from the region, as diplomatic efforts continue amid ongoing conflict.

item.title: Russia Launches Major Nuclear Drills Amid Ukraine War
item.description: Russia began three days of nuclear weapons exercises involving ballistic and cruise missile launches, raising concerns as Belarus simultaneously conducts its own nuclear drills near NATO borders.

item.title: Ukrainian Drone Strikes Deal Double Blow to Russian War Effort
item.description: Medium-range Ukrainian drone attacks on Russian oil refineries have significantly disrupted Russia's war machine, compounding problems from stalled ground offensives and Western sanctions.

item.title: UK Energy Bills Set to Jump 13% as Iran War Drives Gas Prices
item.description: Britain's household energy price cap is expected to surge 13% this summer, the largest increase since 2023, as the conflict in Iran pushes up wholesale gas and electricity costs.

item.title: Polish PM Urges Transatlantic Unity After US Troop Deployment Cancelled
item.description: Donald Tusk stressed the importance of Poland's ties to Washington after the Pentagon cancelled plans to deploy 4,000 US-based troops to Poland, calling for NATO unity during a difficult period.

item.title: Iran Announces New Authority to "Manage" Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran formed a new body to oversee the Strait of Hormuz, managing ship transits and collecting passage fees. The move comes as Tehran continues its blockade while facing a US maritime embargo on Iranian ports.

item.title: Malaysia PM Anwar Eyes Snap Election Before Fuel Subsidy Crisis Bites
item.description: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim is considering a snap election before fuel subsidy costs—potentially 4 billion ringgit monthly due to the Iran war—force spending cuts that would hurt his chances. His coalition is fraying with partners defecting.

item.title: Belarus Holds Nuclear Weapons Drills with Russia
item.description: Belarus began training exercises involving Russian nuclear weapons, drawing strong condemnation from Ukraine amid escalating regional tensions.

item.title: US Sanctions Cuban Officials and Intelligence Agency
item.description: The Trump administration imposed sanctions on 11 Cuban officials including the communications minister and military leaders, escalating pressure on the communist government.

item.title: Taiwan Says China Greatest Source of Regional Instability
item.description: Taiwan condemned China as the primary source of regional unease as Beijing sent a carrier task force into the Western Pacific for training exercises.

item.title: WHO Rejects Taiwan Participation After China Opposition
item.description: World Health Organization member states rejected a proposal to invite Taiwan to its annual assembly in Geneva after China said it would block participation.

item.title: US Drops Adani Cases After $10 Billion Investment Promise
item.description: The Trump administration moved to dismiss criminal fraud charges against Indian billionaire Gautam Adani while settling alleged Iran sanctions violations involving one of his companies.

item.title: Russia strikes Ukrainian Danube port as Kyiv targets Moscow with drones
item.description: Russian air attacks damaged port infrastructure in Izmail, while Russian authorities downed four Ukrainian drones headed toward Moscow in overnight attacks.

item.title: Japan economy grows faster than expected in Q1 despite Iran war headwinds
item.description: Japan posted stronger-than-expected GDP growth in the first quarter driven by solid exports and consumption, though economists warn the Iran war energy shock will test momentum.

item.title: IMF raises UK growth forecast but warns political turmoil threatens outlook
item.description: The International Monetary Fund upgraded Britain's 2026 growth projection but cautioned that domestic political instability could undermine spending and investment.

item.title: Kremlin dismisses Zelenskiy warning about Russian threat to NATO from Belarus
item.description: Moscow rejected Kyiv's assertion that Russia was considering an attack on NATO countries from Belarusian territory, calling the claim unworthy of response.

item.title: Mexico Demands Israel Respect Rights of Gaza Flotilla Nationals
item.description: Mexico called on Israeli authorities to respect the human rights of two Mexican nationals detained after the Israeli Navy intercepted a Gaza-bound aid flotilla. A South Korean activist was also detained.

item.title: Taiwan President Lai Survives First Impeachment Vote
item.description: An impeachment motion against Taiwanese President William Lai failed on Tuesday, marking the first presidential impeachment vote in Taiwans history. The motion was brought by opposition lawmakers.

item.title: Cuba Warns of Bloodbath After US Drone Sanctions
item.description: Cuban President Diaz-Canel warned of a bloodbath if the US attacks, after Washington imposed sanctions following reports Havana obtained over 300 military drones. Cuba accused the US of fabricating drone threat claims.

item.title: Philippines Would Be Involved in Taiwan Conflict, Marcos Says
item.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. acknowledged that due to geographic proximity, the Philippines would likely be involved in any Taiwan conflict. He also said he would discuss Japans defense posture at an upcoming summit amid Taiwan concerns.

item.title: Japan-South Korea to Agree on Oil Stockpiling Cooperation
item.description: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi and South Korean President Lee Jae Myung are set to agree on a cooperative framework for securing crude oil supplies including joint stockpiling at their summit on Tuesday.

item.title: IDF Intercepts Hezbollah Drone Over South Lebanon
item.description: Israeli forces fired an interceptor missile at an apparent Hezbollah drone over southern Lebanon where troops were operating. Israel continues airstrikes in southern Lebanon despite a truce extension.

item.title: Ukrainian Positions in Pokrovsk Nearly Cut Off
item.description: Ukraines 7th Rapid Response Corps says Russian forces control high ground and skies over northern Pokrovsk, making troop rotations and resupply nearly impossible. Analysts say those positions should have been withdrawn months ago.

item.title: Xi to Host Putin in Beijing Days After Trump Visit
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping is set to host Vladimir Putin less than a week after Donald Trump visited Beijing, as China seeks to project itself as a stable global power amid war and energy crisis. The visit underscores Beijing balancing act between Russia and US.

item.title: Pakistan Deploys 8,000 Troops and Fighter Jets to Saudi Arabia
item.description: Pakistan has deployed around 8,000 troops, a squadron of fighter jets and an air defense system to Saudi Arabia under a mutual defense pact during the Iran conflict. The deployment complicates Pakistans role as a neutral mediator in the Iran-US tensions.
